summary:
The issue was that IPv6 loopback addresses (like `::1`) were passing validation, allowing SSRF to the internal metadata service.

To fix this, I updated `app/validator.py` to use Python’s `ipaddress` module. Now, if a hostname is an IP literal (IPv4 or IPv6), the code normalizes it (removing any zone identifiers) and rejects loopback, private, link-local, reserved, or unspecified addresses.

After this change, running `./test.sh` shows all tests passing, confirming the fix.
